title: rubus glandulosus subsp. koehleri
intro: Rubus glandulosus subsp. koehleri, also known as the Himalayan Raspberry, is a subspecies of the raspberry plant native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are thought to contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Rubus glandulosus subsp. koehleri as a medicinal herb.
